meaning: adj.无数的,不可数的n.[语]不可数名词
examples: He has stolen flowers from my parents' grave uncountable times.
A: the word 'money' is uncountable, so you'd say 'much money'. My family is the same as yours.
Every day authors all over the word add millions of words to an uncountable number of overlapping conversations.
But its bed was thick with well-worn river stones, testimony to uncountable seasons of floods and the persuasive nature of water.
In fact, a study in Cuyahoga County, Ohio, found that 10 percent of paper ballots attached to its touchscreen machines were blank, ripped or otherwise uncountable.
